role: Former Minister of Culture and Islamic Guidance (1997–2000); writer; reformist politician
bio: Ataollah Mohajerani served as [Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami)'s Minister of Culture and Islamic Guidance from 1997 to 2000, presiding over the most open period for the Iranian press since the revolution. Under his stewardship, hundreds of new newspapers and magazines were licensed, including the reformist dailies that employed journalists like [Akbar Ganji](/people/akbar-ganji) and [Mashallah Shamsolvaezin](/people/mashallah-shamsolvaezin). He survived a parliamentary impeachment vote in 1999 with Khatami's backing but resigned in 2000 under mounting pressure from hardliners. He co-authored the 2010 [Green Movement](/events/green-movement-protests-2009) manifesto with [Abdolkarim Soroush](/people/abdolkarim-soroush), [Mohsen Kadivar](/people/mohsen-kadivar), and [Akbar Ganji](/people/akbar-ganji). He has lived in exile in London since 2009.
editorial_notes: Born 1954 in Arak. Minister of Culture 1997-2000. Survived impeachment vote 1999. Resigned 2000. Vice President for Legal and Parliamentary Affairs under Khatami briefly. Left Iran 2009. Lives in London. Alive as of April 2026.
